$comment: Hexagonal boundary manifest (Issue #32). Single source of truth for the established persistence, clock/id, and execution ports. Consumed by tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs, which enforces the declared dependency directions. Paths are repo-relative POSIX. This manifest does not claim that every infrastructure concern already has an interchangeable port.
$compositionNote: server.ts and app/context.ts are the application bootstrap composition roots. server.ts is the process entry point and delegates tool registration to src/app/; app/context.ts constructs the application graph and concrete persistence/clock dependencies. execution/registry.ts is the execution feature composition root and constructs the configured local or SSH execution adapters.
$adapterAllowListNote: Completeness guard (repo-wide): tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs scans EVERY src/**/*.ts (not just the hand-maintained consumer lists) and fails if any file imports the persistence adapter (src/db.ts) or node:sqlite unless it is listed here. A new unclassified module that reaches for the adapter — directly or by re-exporting it through a barrel, which still requires importing it — therefore cannot slip through. Keep these lists minimal.
$clockNote: The Clock/IdGenerator ports (src/ports/clock.ts) are injected by the composition root (src/app/context.ts) into these consumers; each depends on the port, not on ambient nowIso/newId. Enforced by tests/architecture-boundary.test.mjs.
$corePortConsumersNote: Infrastructure-independent core services that express infrastructure needs through an established port and may therefore not import the listed concrete I/O modules or adapters. HypothesisRepo is excluded because hypotheses.ts is a repository/DAO. resolve.ts is excluded because it is an application service that imports concrete configuration and repository-boundary validation. The classification proves only the declared boundaries; it does not imply that every infrastructure concern is ported.
$applicationServicesNote: Infrastructure-aware orchestration services. resolve.ts coordinates persistence with concrete configuration and Git repository validation; session.ts coordinates slice lifecycle and execution targets; checks.ts coordinates configured checks and Git diff inspection.
